0c8cc2de-2456-4bd4-b3c2-5d54d6590989.title: Support chat-attached images as direct img2img input for comfyui_generate
0c8cc2de-2456-4bd4-b3c2-5d54d6590989.details:
## Problem
Currently `comfyui_generate` only accepts workspace file paths via `imagePath`. When users attach images in chat (paste/drag-drop), the base64 data lives in memory and is never accessible to ComfyUI. The workaround requires saving the image to disk first, which creates temp file bloat.

## Solution
Pass chat-attached image data directly from memory to ComfyUI's `/upload/image` endpoint via a new `attachedImageIndex` parameter. Zero workspace disk I/O — decode base64 → HTTP multipart upload.

### Changes Required

**1. `internal/tools/types.go`**
- Add `AttachedImage` struct (Name, MediaType, DataURL)
- Add `AttachedImages []AttachedImage` field to `ExecutionContext`

**2. `internal/services/chat.go`**
- Add `latestUserMessageImages(workspaceID string) []tools.AttachedImage` helper that walks the chat session in reverse and returns the latest user message's images (Name, MediaType, DataURL only — no full base64 copy needed beyond what's already there)

**3. `internal/services/file_changes.go`**
- Wire `AttachedImages` into `ExecutionContext` inside `executeTrackedToolCall` before calling `tools.Execute`

**4. `internal/tools/comfyui_generate.go`**
- Add `attachedImageIndex` schema parameter (integer, 0-based)
- Resolution priority: explicit `imagePath` > `attachedImageIndex` with attached images available
- Decode base64 from DataURL → determine extension from MediaType → generate server filename → call `client.UploadImage`
- Update default workflow selection to recognize attached images (routes to img2img workflow when `attachedImageIndex` is used)

**5. System prompt update** (`chat.go` `chatSystemMessage`)
- Add guidance so the model knows about `attachedImageIndex` and that image data passes directly from memory

### Acceptance Criteria
- Attaching an image in chat and running `comfyui_generate` with `attachedImageIndex: 0` produces img2img output using that image as input
- Explicit `imagePath` still takes precedence over attached images
- No temporary files written to workspace disk
- Tests cover: attached image resolution, base64 decode validation, workflow selection routing, index bounds handling
acceptanceCriteria: Chat-attached images accessible via attachedImageIndex parameter without disk I/O
Explicit imagePath takes precedence over attached images
Base64 decode and ComfyUI upload works end-to-end for all supported image formats (PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF)
Default workflow selection routes to img2img when attachedImageIndex is used
System prompt includes guidance for the model about attachedImageIndex
Tests cover resolution priority, decode validation, workflow routing, and index bounds handling

2594572f-7ae7-456c-87d0-4aad4cf01b1b.details:

- Ensure `.terminal-content` has `overflow-y: auto` so internal scrolling works when content exceeds the panel height.

- Scroll to bottom — input row should be fully visible without being cut off by viewport edge

2acc40fa-fb1c-4928-aee0-3ded352e304e.title: Update ComfyUI integration plan: custom workflow support via workflowPath and workflowJSON parameters with template substitution
2acc40fa-fb1c-4928-aee0-3ded352e304e.details:
## Changes to V1 ComfyUI Integration Plan

### Problem
The original plan only supports a hardcoded minimal CLIPTextEncode → KSampler → SaveImage workflow. Users have custom ComfyUI workflows (ControlNet, LoRA, upscaling chains, multi-step pipelines) that need to be usable from Echo.

### Solution — Three Layers

**Layer 1: Prompt substitution in any workflow (automatic)**
When sending *any* workflow JSON to `/prompt`, the client scans for nodes by `class_type` and substitutes template variables into matching inputs:
- `CLIPTextEncode` → `text` input gets `{{PROMPT}}` / `{{NEGATIVE_PROMPT}}`
- `KSampler` / `KSamplerAdvanced` → `seed`, `steps`, `cfg` inputs get substituted
- `EmptyLatentImage` → `width`, `height` inputs get substituted

This means even the default minimal workflow benefits from this — it's not hardcoded node IDs, it's class-type matching.

**Layer 2: Inline workflowJSON parameter**
New tool parameter `workflowJSON string` (optional). If provided, the full ComfyUI workflow JSON is sent instead of the default. Template variables in the JSON are substituted with tool parameters before sending.

**Layer 3: Workflow files from workspace (primary use case)**
New tool parameter `workflowPath string` (optional). Loads a `.json` file from a workspace folder, does template substitution, sends to ComfyUI. This is how users actually work — export workflow from ComfyUI UI → save JSON in workspace → reference by path.

### Updated Tool Parameters

```
comfyui_generate:
  prompt           string    required   — positive text prompt
  negativePrompt   string    optional   — default ""
  workflowPath     string    optional   — workspace-relative .json file; overrides default workflow
  workflowJSON     string    optional   — inline JSON; takes precedence over workflowPath
  model            string    optional   — checkpoint name, substituted as {{MODEL}}
  width            int       optional   — default 512, substituted as {{WIDTH}}
  height           int       optional   — default 512, substituted as {{HEIGHT}}
  steps            int       optional   — default 20, substituted as {{STEPS}}
  cfgScale         number    optional   — default 7.5, substituted as {{CFG_SCALE}}
  seed             int64     optional   — -1 for random, substituted as {{SEED}}
```

### Implementation Changes

**`internal/comfyui/workflow.go`** (new file):
- `LoadWorkflowJSON(path string) (map[string]any, error)` — reads and validates workflow JSON structure
- `SubstituteTemplateVariables(workflow map[string]any, params TemplateParams) map[string]any` — recursive JSON walker that replaces `{{VAR}}` strings in node inputs
- `BuildDefaultWorkflow(params TemplateParams) map[string]any` — builds minimal CLIPTextEncode → KSampler → SaveImage using class-type matching (not hardcoded node IDs)
- `TemplateParams` struct holding all substitutable values

**`internal/comfyui/client.go`** changes:
- `Generate` accepts optional workflow JSON; if nil, calls `BuildDefaultWorkflow`
- POST body uses the substituted workflow directly

**`internal/tools/comfyui_generate.go`** changes:
- Add `workflowPath` and `workflowJSON` to argument struct and JSON schema
- If `workflowPath` provided, read from workspace using existing `ExecutionContext.WorkspaceRoots` path resolution (same as filesystem tools)
- If `workflowJSON` provided, parse directly
- If neither, use built-in default workflow
- Pass resulting workflow JSON to `comfyui.Generate`

**Workspace workflow file format:**
Users export from ComfyUI UI (Manager → Export API or just the raw graph JSON). Template variables are standard double-brace strings:
```json
{
  "3": {
    "class_type": "CLIPTextEncode",
    "inputs": {
      "text": "{{PROMPT}}",
      "clip": "6"
    }
  },
  "5": {
    "class_type": "KSampler",
    "inputs": {
      "seed": "{{SEED}}",
      "steps": "{{STEPS}}",
      "cfg": "{{CFG_SCALE}}"
    }
  },
  "7": {
    "class_type": "EmptyLatentImage",
    "inputs": {
      "width": "{{WIDTH}}",
      "height": "{{HEIGHT}}"
    }
  }
}
```

### User Workflow

1. Build custom pipeline in ComfyUI desktop UI (ControlNet, LoRA, upscale, whatever)
2. Export as JSON with `{{VARIABLE}}` placeholders where dynamic inputs go
3. Save to workspace folder (e.g., `comfy-workflows/face-portrait.json`)
4. In Echo: agent calls `comfyui_generate` with `workflowPath: "echo/comfy-workflows/face-portrait.json"` + prompt
5. Tool loads file → substitutes variables → sends to ComfyUI → returns image

### Security/Validation

- Workflow JSON is validated for basic structure (must have node objects with `class_type`)
- No execution of arbitrary code — ComfyUI handles the workflow execution server-side
- `workflowPath` uses existing workspace path resolution (rejects traversal outside workspace)
- `workflowJSON` size capped at reasonable limit (e.g., 500KB) to prevent abuse

### Testing

- Unit tests for template substitution with various JSON structures
- Test default workflow generation produces valid ComfyUI prompt structure
- Test workflow file loading from workspace path
- Mock ComfyUI server returns known image; verify end-to-end with custom workflow JSON
acceptanceCriteria: Template variable substitution ({{PROMPT}}, {{SEED}}, etc.) works in any ComfyUI workflow JSON structure
workflowPath parameter loads .json files from workspace folders using existing path resolution
workflowJSON parameter accepts inline JSON that overrides default workflow
Default minimal workflow uses class-type matching (CLIPTextEncode, KSampler, EmptyLatentImage) not hardcoded node IDs
Users can export custom workflows from ComfyUI UI, add template variables, and use them via workflowPath
go test ./... passes with new workflow.go tests

4367bd5c-c699-49c1-b20c-3172414b431c.title: Fix 413 Request Entity Too Large from accumulated image data in LLM requests
4367bd5c-c699-49c1-b20c-3172414b431c.details:
## Problem

After a tool returns an image (e.g., `filesystem_read_image`, ComfyUI generate), the base64-encoded data URL is included in `ContentParts` on the tool result message. This message is appended to both the in-memory `messages` slice AND persistent chat history via `appendChatHistory`. On every subsequent LLM request, all accumulated image-bearing messages are serialized into the JSON body — a single generated image can be several MB of base64. After a few turns, the payload exceeds nginx's `client_max_body_size` → **413 Request Entity Too Large**.

The error occurs on the *next* turn after successful image generation because that's when the previously-stored image-bearing history is included again in the request body.

## Root Cause Trace

1. `toolResultImageMessage` (`internal/services/tool_images.go:29`) creates a message with `ContentParts` containing the full base64 data URL
2. `runChatTurnWithHistory` (`internal/services/chat.go:599-601`) appends these messages to both in-memory `messages` AND persistent history
3. On every subsequent turn, all image-bearing messages are serialized into the request body

## Fix

Strip image/video `ContentParts` from tool result messages before storing them in persistent chat history. Keep only the text description (e.g., "Image returned by tool filesystem_read_image: output.png (image/png, 2.3 MB).").

### 1. `internal/services/tool_images.go` — Add helper

```go
func stripMediaContentParts(message llm.Message) llm.Message {
    if len(message.ContentParts) == 0 {
        return message
    }
    // Keep the text content, discard image/video parts
    return llm.Message{
        Role:    message.Role,
        Content: message.Content,
    }
}
```

### 2. `internal/services/chat.go` — Strip before storing in history (line ~600-601)

Change:
```go
for _, resultMessage := range execution.Messages {
    s.appendChatHistory(workspace.ID, resultMessage)
}
```

To:
```go
for _, resultMessage := range execution.Messages {
    stripped := stripMediaContentParts(resultMessage)
    s.appendChatHistory(workspace.ID, stripped)
}
```

### 3. Verify context compaction also strips images

Check `internal/services/context_compaction.go` (or wherever compaction logic lives) to ensure forced compaction also strips image ContentParts so they don't survive a compaction pass.

### 4. Test — `internal/services/system_test.go` or new test file

Add test that verifies image data URLs don't persist in chat history after tool execution, extending the existing `TestVisibleChatHistoryPreservesRemainingImages`.

## Why this works

- The LLM still sees the image **during the turn it was generated** (the `messages` slice includes it for the current request)
- On subsequent turns, only the text description persists (~100 bytes vs. megabytes of base64)
- User-visible chat messages (`ChatMessage.Images`) are unaffected — images still display in the UI
acceptanceCriteria: Image data URLs are not stored in persistent chat history after tool execution
Text descriptions of images remain in history for LLM context
User-visible image attachments in the UI are unaffected
Context compaction also strips image ContentParts to prevent accumulation
go test ./... passes

438fdf39-5966-4311-8c53-85bb04014bcd.title: Wire generatedImages map through kanban agent tool execution loop
438fdf39-5966-4311-8c53-85bb04014bcd.details:
## Problem

Kanban agents cannot chain `comfyui_generate` → `save_image` because `generatedImages` is never passed to their tool calls. `executeKanbanToolCall` (line 793 of `kanban_scheduler.go`) calls `executeTrackedToolCall(..., nil, nil)` — the last `nil` is for `generatedImages`.

## Fix

### `internal/services/kanban_scheduler.go`

**1. Add `generatedImages` to the kanban agent loop** (around line 430, where `messages`, `recoverableToolCalls`, etc. are declared):

```go
generatedImages := make(map[string]tools.AttachedImage)
```

**2. Pass it through in `executeKanbanToolCall`** (line 793):

Change:
```go
execution := s.executeTrackedToolCall(ctx, workspace, settings, call, WorkspaceChangeSource{...}, func(event tools.Event) {...}, nil, nil)
```

To:
```go
execution := s.executeTrackedToolCall(ctx, workspace, settings, call, WorkspaceChangeSource{...}, func(event tools.Event) {...}, nil, generatedImages)
```

**3. Populate `generatedImages` after image-producing tool calls** (after the tool result processing, around line 810):

Copy the same logic from `chat.go` lines 864-882 that extracts ImageID from tool results and adds them to the map:

```go
// Track images produced by tools so subsequent tool calls can reference them.
if image, ok := toolResultImageMessage(call.Function.Name, result); ok {
    if idProvider, ok := result.Output.(tools.ImageIDProvider); ok && idProvider.ImageID() != "" {
        generatedImages[idProvider.ImageID()] = tools.AttachedImage{
            Name:      image.Name,
            MediaType: image.MediaType,
            DataURL:   image.DataURL,
        }
    } else if outMap, jsonOk := result.Output.(map[string]any); jsonOk {
        if imageID, ok := outMap["imageId"].(string); ok && imageID != "" {
            generatedImages[imageID] = tools.AttachedImage{
                Name:      image.Name,
                MediaType: image.MediaType,
                DataURL:   image.DataURL,
            }
        }
    }
}
```

Wait — `toolResultImageMessage` is in `chat.go` / `tool_images.go`. Need to check if it's accessible from `kanban_scheduler.go` (same package? No, different files but same `services` package).

Actually, looking at the code: both `chat.go` and `kanban_scheduler.go` are in `internal/services/`, so they share the package. The helper `toolResultImageMessage` is likely in `tool_images.go` which is also in `internal/services/`. So it should be directly callable.

### Verification

- `go test ./...` passes
- Kanban agent can call `comfyui_generate` then `save_image` with the imageId from the generation result

generatedImages passed to executeTrackedToolCall instead of nil
Image-producing tool results populate generatedImages after execution (same pattern as chat.go lines 864-882)

5204ac68-1a0a-4299-af99-25c220a58e06.title: ComfyUI model/workflow discovery and default checkpoint settings
5204ac68-1a0a-4299-af99-25c220a58e06.details:
## Problem

`comfyui_generate` uses a hardcoded checkpoint name (`"checkpoint1"`) when no model is specified, causing opaque "execution status is error" failures. Users have no way to discover available models or workflows on their ComfyUI server, and Echo stores nothing locally that would help.

## Approach

Use **ComfyUI-Manager's built-in API** (already installed on the server) for discovery. No local storage of workflows — everything stays on the ComfyUI machine. On-demand fetches only.

### ComfyUI-Manager endpoints to use:
- `GET /api/manage/models` — lists checkpoint/LoRA/VAE/CLIP models with metadata (filename, size, hash)
- `POST /api/manage/workflows/list` — lists saved workflows (names + descriptions)
- `POST /api/manage/workflows/get?workflow=<name>` — fetches workflow JSON on-demand by name

### Changes by file:

#### Backend — `internal/comfyui/client.go`
- Add `ListModels(ctx) ([]ModelInfo, error)` — queries `/api/manage/models`, parses checkpoint entries into typed list

5fb4ce1e-80a8-4704-a4d2-247e0b067160.title: Handle unexpected EOF gracefully in SSE stream parser when partial content received
5fb4ce1e-80a8-4704-a4d2-247e0b067160.details:
In `echo/internal/llm/stream.go`, when the LLM endpoint closes the connection without a proper SSE terminator (`[DONE]` or `finish_reason`), the scanner returns `io.ErrUnexpectedEOF`. This is treated as a hard `EventError` even if partial content was already streamed.

When EOF occurs:
- If `completed` is already true (a `[DONE]` or `finish_reason` was received), treat EOF as harmless — no event needed
- If content/tool-calls were partially streamed, emit an `EventComplete` instead of an `EventError` so the partial response is usable
- Only emit `EventError` for EOF if no data was received at all
acceptanceCriteria: EOF after [DONE] or finish_reason is silently ignored (no error event)
EOF with partial content emits EventComplete instead of EventError
EOF with no data received still emits EventError
Add tests in stream_test.go for all three EOF scenarios

74958278-c454-45f2-b29e-cfbb7a844b72.title: ComfyUI integration: remote endpoint, agent tool, settings, and chat image display
74958278-c454-45f2-b29e-cfbb7a844b72.details:
## Overview

Add ComfyUI as a remote endpoint in Echo — same architectural pattern as the SearXNG web search integration. Users configure a ComfyUI host URL in settings; agents call a `comfyui_generate` tool to produce images; results render inline in chat via the existing `ChatImageAttachment` path.

## Architecture

### 1. Settings (`internal/llm/settings.go`)

Add `ComfyuiURL string` to the `Settings` struct, parallel to `SearxngURL`.

- Add `DefaultComfyuiURL = "http://127.0.0.1:8188"` constant
- Normalize in `Settings.Normalized()` (trim, fallback to default if empty — but allow empty to mean "disabled")
- Validate HTTP URL format in `Settings.Validate()` only when non-empty
- Clone copies it

### 2. ComfyUI client package (`internal/comfyui/`)

New package mirroring `internal/searxng/`:

**`client.go`** — HTTP client for the ComfyUI API:
- `Generate(ctx, baseURL, prompt, negativePrompt, model, width, height, steps, cfgScale, seed) (*GenerationResult, error)`
- POST to `/prompt` with a minimal workflow JSON (CLIPTextEncode → KSampler → SaveImage or PreviewImage)
- Returns prompt ID for queue tracking

**`queue.go`** — WebSocket progress/result polling:
- `WaitForCompletion(ctx, baseURL, promptID) (*GenerationResult, error)`
- Connects to `/ws?clientId=<uuid>`, listens for execution_start/execution_cached/executing/status events
- On `executing` with null payload = generation done; fetch result from `/view?filename=&subfolder=&type=result`
- Returns base64 image data and format

**`types.go`** — Request/response models:
- `GenerationRequest`, `GenerationResult`, `WorkflowPrompt`

### 3. Agent tool (`internal/tools/comfyui_generate.go`)

Follows the `web_search.go` pattern exactly:

```go
func init() {
    Register(ToolFunc{
        Meta: Metadata{
            Name:        "comfyui_generate",
            Description: "Generate an image using a remote ComfyUI Stable Diffusion instance.",
            Parameters: Schema{...},
        },
        Run: comfyuiGenerate,
    })
}
```

**Parameters:**
- `prompt` (string, required) — positive text prompt
- `negativePrompt` (string, optional)
- `model` (string, optional) — checkpoint name
- `width` (integer, default 512)
- `height` (integer, default 512)
- `steps` (integer, default 20)
- `cfgScale` (number, default 7.5)
- `seed` (integer, optional; -1 for random)

**ExecutionContext dependency:** Needs a new field `ComfyuiURL string` on `ExecutionContext`, populated from settings when building the context in chat.go/kanban_scheduler.go.

**Output:** Returns `ComfyuiGenerateOutput` with:
- `Prompt string` — the prompt used
- `ImageDataURL string` — base64 data URL of generated image
- `Seed int64` — seed actually used
- `Steps int` — steps taken
- `GenerationTimeMs int` — wall-clock time

**Tool classification:** Mutating (produces a side-effect on the ComfyUI server). Add to `mutatingToolNames`.

### 4. Chat integration (`internal/services/chat.go`)

- Pass `settings.ComfyuiURL` into `ExecutionContext.ComfyuiURL` when building tool execution context
- When `comfyui_generate` returns, the result image data URL can be attached to the assistant message as a `ChatImageAttachment` (same path as pasted images) — or simply rendered inline in the markdown output

### 5. Settings UI (`frontend/src/app/settings/index.ts`)

Add ComfyUI section in settings form, parallel to SearXNG:
- Text input for `comfyuiUrl` with label "ComfyUI Host URL"
- Placeholder `http://127.0.0.1:8188`
- Help text: "URL of your ComfyUI instance. Leave empty to disable."
- Save/load in the existing settings save flow

### 6. Web access (`internal/webserver/server.go`)

If new `SystemService` methods are added for ComfyUI status/testing, they need explicit RPC entry. The tool approach doesn't require this since tools execute through the agent loop, not direct RPC.

## Files to Create/Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `internal/comfyui/client.go` | New — HTTP client for `/prompt` API |
| `internal/comfyui/queue.go` | New — WebSocket progress polling |
| `internal/comfyui/types.go` | New — request/response types |
| `internal/comfyui/client_test.go` | New — httptest + mock WS server tests |
| `internal/llm/settings.go` | Modify — add `ComfyuiURL` field, normalize, validate |
| `internal/tools/comfyui_generate.go` | New — tool registration and handler |
| `internal/tools/comfyui_generate_test.go` | New — unit tests |
| `internal/tools/registry.go` | Modify — add to `mutatingToolNames`, `isPathArgKey` if needed |
| `internal/tools/types.go` | Modify — add `ComfyuiURL` to `ExecutionContext` |
| `internal/services/chat.go` | Modify — pass ComfyUI URL into ExecutionContext |
| `internal/services/kanban_scheduler.go` | Modify — pass ComfyUI URL into ExecutionContext |
| `frontend/src/app/settings/index.ts` | Modify — add ComfyUI URL input field |

## Key Design Decisions

1. **No node graph UI** — Echo is a remote control, not a ComfyUI replacement. The tool sends a minimal text-to-image workflow. Advanced workflows come later if needed.
2. **Minimal workflow by default** — CLIPTextEncode (+) → KSampler → SaveImage. Users with custom workflows can extend via a `workflowJSON` parameter in V2.
3. **WebSocket polling, not long-polling** — ComfyUI's WS API is the standard way to track generation progress and get results.
4. **Seed handling** -1 means random; otherwise use exact seed for reproducibility.
5. **Timeout** — Generation can take 30s-5min depending on model/hardware. Use a generous default timeout (300s) configurable per-generation, separate from LLM timeout.

## Testing Strategy

- `internal/comfyui/` tests use `httptest` for the HTTP endpoints and a mock WebSocket server
- Tool tests verify argument validation, URL passthrough, and output shaping
- Integration test: mock ComfyUI server returns a known PNG; tool returns correct data URL
- No live model calls in tests

## V2 Scope (not now)

- Custom workflow JSON parameter
- Image-to-image, inpainting, upscale workflows
- Model/list endpoint to show available checkpoints
- Generation history gallery
- Progress streaming to chat UI

8b08ab98-547e-4628-b0b1-7f1f7f35f067.title: Improve token estimation for base64 data URLs in context compaction
8b08ab98-547e-4628-b0b1-7f1f7f35f067.details:
## Problem

The context compaction (`internal/services/context_compaction.go`) uses a flat `1600` tokens per image regardless of actual base64 size. This is a massive underestimate: a 2 MB image produces ~2.7 MB of base64 text, which at chars/3 = ~900K tokens — not 1600.

This means compaction triggers too late (or not at all) when images are present in history, and the actual JSON wire size far exceeds what the endpoint can accept.

## Fix

**File:** `internal/services/context_compaction.go`

In `estimateContextMessageTokens`, instead of adding a flat `contextCompactionImageTokens` constant, count the actual characters in the base64 data URL:

```go
if part.ImageURL != nil && part.ImageURL.URL != "" {
    total += estimateContextTextTokens(part.ImageURL.URL)
}
```

This counts every character of the base64 string at chars/3, giving a much more accurate token estimate. The flat 1600 constant can be removed.

## Impact

- Context compaction will trigger earlier when images are present
- The estimated token count will match actual wire size much more closely
- Fewer surprise 413 errors from accumulated image data

## Verification
- `go test ./...` passes
- Token estimate for a message with a 2MB base64 image is ~900K tokens, not 1600

951da82b-0462-494a-906c-b9473151e09d.title: Add save_image agent tool for saving generated images to workspace disk
951da82b-0462-494a-906c-b9473151e09d.details:
## Problem
When `comfyui_generate` returns an image, the agent sees only metadata (name, mediaType, bytes) in its JSON response — not the base64 data. The `dataURL` field on `comfyuiOutput` is unexported and never serialized to the model. There's no way for the agent to save generated images because:

1. Agent can't see the image binary data
2. No tool exists to write binary files (`filesystem_create_text` only handles text)
3. No way to reference previously-generated images across tool calls within a turn

## Solution: `save_image` tool

### 1. Expose ImageID in comfyui_generate output
**File: `internal/tools/comfyui_generate.go`**
- Add exported `ImageID string json:"imageId"` field to `comfyuiOutput`
- Generate a unique ID (e.g., `uuid.New().String()`) when image data is fetched
- Set `output.ImageID` alongside Name/MediaType/Bytes

### 2. Track generated images in ExecutionContext
**File: `internal/tools/types.go`**
- Add `GeneratedImages map[string]AttachedImage` to `ExecutionContext` — tracks images produced by tools during the current turn, keyed by ImageID

### 3. Wire generated images into execution context
**File: `internal/services/chat.go`** (in `runChatTurnWithHistory`)
- When building `ExecutionContext` for tool calls within a turn, populate `GeneratedImages` from images attached to the current assistant message (from previous tool results)
- This allows multi-turn chains: generate image → save_image with imageId

### 4. Create `save_image` tool
**File: `internal/tools/save_image.go`** (new)

```go
func init() {
    Register(ToolFunc{
        Meta: Metadata{
            Name: "save_image",
            Description: "Save an image to the workspace. Use this after generating images with comfyui_generate to persist them to disk.",
            Parameters: Schema{...},
        },
        Run: saveImage,
    })
}
```

Parameters:
- `imageId` (string, required) — ImageID from a previous tool result (e.g., comfyui_generate output)
- `path` (string, required) — Workspace-relative path to save the image to
- `overwrite` (bool, optional) — Whether to replace an existing file

Logic:
1. Look up `imageId` in `ctx.GeneratedImages`
2. Decode base64 from DataURL to raw bytes
3. Resolve workspace path via `resolveWorkspaceChildPath(ctx, args.Path)`
4. Write binary file using `os.WriteFile` (same pattern as `filesystem_create_text`)
5. Record file change for tracking

### 5. Add save_image to mutating tools
**File: `internal/tools/registry.go`**
- Add `"save_image"` to `mutatingToolNames`
- Add `"path"` to `isPathArgKey` switch

### 6. Update system prompt guidance
**File: `internal/services/chat.go`** (`chatSystemMessage`)
- Add guidance so the model knows about `save_image` and that it should use the `imageId` from tool results

## Verification
- Agent sees `"imageId": "uuid..."` in comfyui_generate JSON response
- Generated images are available in subsequent tool calls within the same turn
- save_image writes binary file correctly to workspace disk
- File change tracking records the creation

a71f0693-d889-4a8d-8b2b-c09cd5384c96.title: Strip media ContentParts from research bootstrap results in chat.go
a71f0693-d889-4a8d-8b2b-c09cd5384c96.details:
## Problem

In `runChatTurnWithHistory` (line 608-610), the research bootstrap tool execution appends raw (unstripped) result messages to both `messages` and history:

```go
messages = append(messages, execution.Messages...)
for _, resultMessage := range execution.Messages {
    s.appendChatHistory(workspace.ID, resultMessage)  // <- raw, not stripped!
}
```

Compare to the **main tool loop** (line 857-861) which correctly strips:

```go
for _, resultMessage := range execution.Messages {
    stripped := stripMediaContentParts(resultMessage)
    messages = append(messages, stripped)
    s.appendChatHistory(workspace.ID, stripped)
}
```

If a research tool returns media content (e.g., `filesystem_read_image`), the full base64 DataURL gets appended to `session.History` unstripped in the bootstrap path. This could cause 413 errors on subsequent LLM calls within the same turn if multiple image-producing tools run during bootstrap.

## Fix

**File:** `internal/services/chat.go`, lines 608-610

Change:
```go
messages = append(messages, execution.Messages...)
for _, resultMessage := range execution.Messages {
    s.appendChatHistory(workspace.ID, resultMessage)
}
```

To:
```go
for _, resultMessage := range execution.Messages {
    stripped := stripMediaContentParts(resultMessage)
    messages = append(messages, stripped)
    s.appendChatHistory(workspace.ID, stripped)
}
```

## Verification
- `go test ./...` passes
- Research bootstrap with image-producing tools doesn't accumulate base64 data in history

## Background

cc6b72d7-20a4-41e7-9336-81ec104b97df.title: Add configurable request body size limit with warning threshold in LLM client
cc6b72d7-20a4-41e7-9336-81ec104b97df.details:
## Problem

The LLM client sends requests without any body size guard. If context compaction fails to trigger (due to estimation bugs) or history accumulates unexpectedly, the request can grow until the endpoint rejects it with 413.

## Fix

### `internal/llm/client.go`

Add a hard request body size limit that triggers forced action before sending:

```go
const defaultMaxRequestBodyBytes = 8 * 1024 * 1024 // 8 MB

// After JSON marshaling in both Complete() and StreamChat():
if len(body) > maxRequestBodyBytes {
    log.Printf("ERROR: LLM request body %d bytes exceeds limit of %d bytes. Truncating history.", len(body), maxRequestBodyBytes)
    // Return a structured error so the chat layer can trigger forced compaction
    return ChatCompletion{Error: &requestTooLargeError{actual: len(body)}}
}
```

### `internal/llm/settings.go`

Make the limit configurable via settings:

```go
type Settings struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    MaxRequestBodyBytes int `json:"maxRequestBodyBytes,omitempty"` // 0 = use default (8 MB)
}
```

### `internal/services/chat.go`

When the LLM client returns a `requestTooLargeError`, trigger forced compaction on the session history before retrying:

```go
if errors.As(err, &requestTooLargeError{}) {
    s.forceCompactSession(workspace.ID)
    // Retry the request once after compaction
}
```

## Why this matters

- **Last line of defense**: Even if token estimation is wrong and compaction doesn't trigger, the hard limit catches oversized requests.
- **Self-healing**: Returning a structured error lets chat.go retry after forced compaction instead of showing a raw 413 to the user.
- **Configurable**: Users with generous endpoint limits can raise the threshold.

## Files
- `internal/llm/client.go` — body size check in Complete() and StreamChat()
- `internal/llm/settings.go` — configurable limit field
- `internal/services/chat.go` — retry logic on requestTooLargeError

## Verification
- `go test ./...` passes
- Requests exceeding the limit return structured error instead of 413

d3d1b58f-ea87-4ae2-acb1-76de08ead777.title: Compress chat image attachments before sending to LLM endpoint
d3d1b58f-ea87-4ae2-acb1-76de08ead777.details:
## Problem

Chat messages with large image attachments (e.g. 1.1 MB PNG screenshots) cause **413 Request Entity Too Large** errors from nginx sitting in front of the LLM endpoint. The root cause: images are sent as raw base64 data URLs with zero compression or resizing. A 1.1 MB PNG inflates to ~1.5 MB base64, exceeding nginx's default `client_max_body_size` (typically 1 MB).

Most vision models don't need full-resolution images — 2048px on the longest side is more than sufficient.

## Changes

### 1. Add image processing dependency (`go.mod`)
- Add `github.com/disintegration/imaging` for resize support

### 2. Backend compression (`internal/services/chat_images.go`)
- Add constants: `maxImageDimension = 2048`, `jpegQuality = 85`
- Create `compressChatImage(rawData []byte, mediaType string) ([]byte, string, error)` that:
  - Decodes via `image.Decode`
  - Resizes proportionally if either dimension exceeds `maxImageDimension` (using `imaging.Resize`)
  - For PNG files > ~500KB after resizing, re-encode as JPEG at quality 85
  - For GIF files, keep as-is to preserve animation
  - Returns compressed bytes and potentially updated media type
- Apply compression in **both** entry points:
  - `normalizePastedChatImage`: After `parseChatImageDataURL` returns raw data
  - `readWorkspaceChatImage`: After `os.ReadFile` returns file bytes

### 3. Better 413 error messaging (`internal/llm/client.go`)
- In `responseError`, detect HTTP 413 status codes and return a user-friendly message: *"LLM endpoint rejected the request (413 Request Entity Too Large). This is usually caused by large image attachments or accumulated context. Try using smaller images."*

### 4. Optional: Browser-side pre-compression (`frontend/src/app/chat/index.ts`)
- When preparing chat image drafts, check decoded image dimensions
- Use Canvas API to resize + re-encode as JPEG/WebP if needed
- Reduces bandwidth frontend ↔ backend (important for web access mode)

## Testing
- Add unit tests in `internal/services` for `compressChatImage`:
  - Small images pass through unchanged
  - Large PNGs get resized and optionally re-encoded as JPEG
  - GIFs are preserved as-is
  - Verify resulting data URL is valid and under expected size
acceptanceCriteria: Large PNG screenshots (1-5 MB) are resized to max 2048px and re-encoded as JPEG, reducing base64 payload by 70%+
GIF attachments are preserved without lossy compression
Small images (<500KB, <2048px) pass through with minimal overhead
413 errors from nginx produce a user-friendly error message instead of raw HTML
compression is applied to both pasted and workspace-referenced (@path) images
No new external dependencies beyond github.com/disintegration/imaging

d5e8afae-794d-44da-a9a8-e313350d6e7f.title: Fix save_image "image not found" error
d5e8afae-794d-44da-a9a8-e313350d6e7f.details:
## Problem

When the agent calls `save_image` after `comfyui_generate`, it fails with:
```
image with ID "..." was not found; it may have been generated in a previous turn
```

## Root Causes (2 bugs)

### Bug 1: `save_image` missing from frontend per-tool permission selector

**File:** `frontend/src/app/settings/index.ts` (~line 117)

The `availableToolNames` array doesn't include `"save_image"`. Custom agent modes can't grant it via the UI, so agents with restricted tool scopes get `tool_not_allowed`.

**Fix:** Add `"save_image"` to `availableToolNames` in alphabetical order (after `"restart"`).

### Bug 2: Image tracking may silently fail; error message gives no diagnostics

**Files:**
- `internal/tools/save_image.go` (~line 78) — error message
- `internal/services/chat.go` (~lines 856-882) — image tracking
- `internal/services/kanban_scheduler.go` (~lines 814-834) — image tracking

The `"image not found"` error is useless for debugging. It doesn't tell you whether:
- The tracking code never ran (type assertion to `LLMImageContentProvider` failed)
- ComfyUI returned zero output images (`ImageID` stays empty, nothing tracked)
- The agent passed a wrong/mangled ID

**Fixes:**

1. **Enhance error message** in `save_image.go` — when image not found, include list of available image IDs so the agent can compare:
   ```go
   return nil, SafeError{Code: "image_not_found", Message: fmt.Sprintf("image with ID %q was not found. Available image IDs: %v", args.ImageID, keys)}
   ```

2. **Add logging** in both `chat.go` and `kanban_scheduler.go` image tracking blocks — log when an image is successfully tracked vs when type assertions fail, so you can see if the tracking code path executed.

3. **Handle zero-output-images case** — When ComfyUI returns no output images, `comfyui_generate` sets neither `ImageID` nor `dataURL`. The image tracking code correctly skips this (both checks fail), but the agent sees a successful result with no `imageId` and doesn't know why. Consider adding guidance to the system prompt: "If comfyui_generate returns no imageId, the generation produced no images — check outputImages and statusMessage for details."

## Tests

Add unit test in `internal/tools/save_image_test.go`:
- Happy path: generate mock image → track in GeneratedImages → save successfully
- Image not found: verify error includes available IDs  
- Empty data URL: verify error is returned
acceptanceCriteria: save_image added to frontend availableToolNames array
Error message includes list of available image IDs when lookup fails
Image tracking logs diagnostics when type assertions fail or succeed
Unit test covers happy path and failure cases for save_image

f3aeeba2-118a-4335-b78d-211d1b439c7a.title: Add save_image agent tool for saving generated images to workspace disk
f3aeeba2-118a-4335-b78d-211d1b439c7a.details:
## Problem
When `comfyui_generate` returns, the agent sees metadata (name, mediaType, bytes) but cannot save the image because:
- `dataURL` is unexported and never serialized to JSON
- No binary file write tool exists
- No way to reference previously-generated images across tool calls

## Solution

### 1. Expose ImageID in comfyui_generate output
**File:** `internal/tools/comfyui_generate.go`
- Add exported `ImageID string json:"imageId"` field to `comfyuiOutput`
- Generate a unique ID (e.g., `uuid.New().String()`) when image data is fetched
- Set `output.ImageID` alongside Name/MediaType/Bytes

### 2. Track generated images in ExecutionContext  
**File:** `internal/tools/types.go`
- Add `GeneratedImages map[string]AttachedImage` to `ExecutionContext` — tracks images produced by tools during the current turn, keyed by ImageID

### 3. Wire generated images into execution context
**File:** `internal/services/chat.go` (in `runChatTurnWithHistory`)
- When building `ExecutionContext` for tool calls within a turn, populate `GeneratedImages` from images attached to the current assistant message (from previous tool results)
- This allows multi-turn chains: generate image → save_image with imageId

### 4. Create `save_image` tool
**File:** `internal/tools/save_image.go` (new)

```go
func init() {
    Register(ToolFunc{
        Meta: Metadata{
            Name: "save_image",
            Description: "Save an image to the workspace. Use this after generating images with comfyui_generate to persist them to disk.",
            Parameters: Schema{...},
        },
        Run: saveImage,
    })
}
```

Parameters:
- `imageId` (string, required) — ImageID from a previous tool result (e.g., comfyui_generate output)
- `path` (string, required) — Workspace-relative path to save the image to  
- `overwrite` (bool, optional) — Whether to replace an existing file

Logic:
1. Look up `imageId` in `ctx.GeneratedImages`
2. Decode base64 from DataURL to raw bytes
3. Resolve workspace path via `resolveWorkspaceChildPath(ctx, args.Path)`
4. Write binary file using `os.WriteFile` (same pattern as `filesystem_create_text`)
5. Record file change for tracking

### 5. Add save_image to mutating tools
**File:** `internal/tools/registry.go`
- Add `"save_image"` to `mutatingToolNames`
- Add `"path"` to `isPathArgKey` switch

### 6. Update system prompt guidance
**File:** `internal/services/chat.go` (`chatSystemMessage`)
- Add guidance so the model knows about `save_image` and that it should use the `imageId` from tool results

## Verification
- Agent sees `"imageId": "uuid..."` in comfyui_generate JSON response
- Generated images are available in subsequent tool calls within the same turn
- save_image writes binary file correctly to workspace disk
- File change tracking records the creation

f40ad6a7-6704-4c25-8065-4b6a3f53e808.title: Force-strip image ContentParts from persisted chat history on session restore
f40ad6a7-6704-4c25-8065-4b6a3f53e808.details:
## Problem

The user reports 413 errors even when their current message has no image attachments. The root cause: **base64 image data URLs persist in `session.History` across turns** from previous tool results (e.g., `filesystem_read_image`, `comfyui_generate`). Even after the P0 fix strips images on *new* tool results, existing persisted history still contains full base64 payloads.

When a chat session is restored from `state.json`, all historical image-bearing messages are loaded with their full data URLs intact. On every subsequent LLM request, these accumulated megabytes of base64 inflate the JSON body. Since token estimation uses a flat 1600 tokens/image instead of actual base64 length, compaction never triggers before hitting the endpoint's size limit.

## Fix

### Option A: Strip on history load (recommended)

In `internal/services/state_persistence.go` or wherever chat sessions are restored, run `stripMediaContentParts` over all history messages after loading:

```go
func stripHistoryMedia(session *ChatSession) {
    for i := range session.History {
        session.History[i] = stripMediaContentParts(session.History[i])
    }
}
```

Call this in the session restoration path (likely `restoreSessions` or equivalent).

### Option B: Strip on state.json load

In the state deserialization, walk all workspaces → sessions → history and strip. This is a one-time migration that runs every startup.

### Option C: Both A and hard body size guard

Add both the strip-on-load AND a hard request body size check in `internal/llm/client.go`:

```go
const maxRequestBodyBytes = 4 * 1024 * 1024 // 4 MB

// After marshaling...
if len(body) > maxRequestBodyBytes {
    log.Printf("WARN: LLM request body %d bytes exceeds limit, truncating history", len(body))
    // Trigger forced compaction or drop oldest messages
}
```

## Why this matters

- **Immediate relief**: Existing persisted sessions with accumulated image data will be cleaned on next app restart.
- **Prevention**: New tool results are already stripped by the P0 bootstrap fix, so no new base64 enters history.
- **Defense in depth**: The body size guard catches any edge cases where compaction fails to trigger.

## Files
- `internal/services/state_persistence.go` — session restoration
- `internal/services/chat.go` — `stripMediaContentParts` already exists
- `internal/llm/client.go` — optional body size guard

## Verification
- Load a persisted session with historical image tool results
- Verify history messages have no ImageURL ContentParts after restore
- `go test ./...` passes
